responses.local: Je vous recommande ce sirop et ces comprimés.

responses.phonetic: ah-VAY voo duh lah FYEV-ruh
responses.local: Avez-vous de la fièvre ?

phrases.intent_en: Ask for something for a fever
phrases.text_local: J'ai de la fièvre. Vous avez quelque chose ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhay duh lah FYEV-ruh. vooz ah-VAY kel-kuh SHOZE
phrases.cultural_note: Paracetamol (Doliprane) is the standard first choice in France. Ibuprofen (Advil, Nurofen) is also common. Aspirin is less routinely recommended.

responses.phonetic: doo doh-lee-PRAHN toot lay see ZUR
responses.local: Du Doliprane toutes les six heures.

responses.phonetic: kel eh voh-truh tahm-pay-rah-TOOR
responses.local: Quelle est votre température ?

phrases.intent_en: Ask for something for diarrhoea or nausea
phrases.text_local: J'ai la diarrhée / des nausées. Qu'est-ce que vous recommandez ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhay lah dyah-RAY / day noh-ZAY. kess kuh voo ruh-koh-mahn-DAY
phrases.cultural_note: For diarrhoea: Imodium (lopéramide) or Smecta (clay-based). For nausea: Primperan or Vogalib. Stay hydrated — rehydration salts ('sels de réhydratation') are also available.

responses.english: Take Imodium and drink plenty of water.
responses.phonetic: pruh-NAY duh loh-dee-UM ay boo-VAY boh-KOO DOH
responses.local: Prenez de l'Imodium et buvez beaucoup d'eau.

responses.english: How long have you had these symptoms?
responses.phonetic: duh-PWEE kahn ah-VAY voo seh samp-TOHM
responses.local: Depuis quand avez-vous ces symptômes ?

phrases.text_local: J'ai besoin de bandages et d'antiseptique.
phrases.pronunciation: zhay buh-ZWAHN duh bahn-DAZH ay dahn-tee-sep-TEEK
phrases.cultural_note: French pharmacies stock full wound care supplies. Common brands: Bétadine (antiseptic), Steri-Strip (closure strips), Compeed (blister plasters).

responses.phonetic: vwah-LAH suh keel voo FOH
responses.local: Voilà ce qu'il vous faut.

responses.phonetic: lah bleh-SOOR eh proh-FOHND
responses.local: La blessure est profonde ?

phrases.text_local: J'ai une réaction allergique. Vous avez un antihistaminique ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhay oon ray-ak-SYOHN ah-lair-ZHEEK. vooz ah-VAY uhn ahn-tee-ees-tah-mee-NEEK
phrases.cultural_note: Common antihistamines in France: Zyrtec (cetirizine), Clarityne (loratadine), Aerius (desloratadine). Pharmacists can advise on drowsy vs non-drowsy options.

responses.english: Here's Zyrtec — take it once a day.
responses.phonetic: vwah-SEE doo zeer-TEK — ah PRAHN-druh oon fwah par ZHOOR
responses.local: Voici du Zyrtec — à prendre une fois par jour.

responses.phonetic: kel sohn voh samp-TOHM ex-ak-tuh-MAHN
responses.local: Quels sont vos symptômes exactement ?

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to take a medicine
phrases.text_local: Comment je dois prendre ce médicament ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MAHN zhuh DWAH PRAHN-druh suh may-dee-kah-MAHN
phrases.cultural_note: French pharmacists always explain dosage. Key words: 'comprimé' (tablet), 'gélule' (capsule), 'fois par jour' (times per day), 'à jeun' (on an empty stomach), 'avec de l'eau' (with water).

responses.english: One tablet three times a day, with water.
responses.phonetic: uhn kohm-pree-MAY twah fwah par ZHOOR, ah-VEK duh LOH
responses.local: Un comprimé trois fois par jour, avec de l'eau.

responses.phonetic: ah PRAHN-druh pahn-DAHN lay ruh-PAH
responses.local: À prendre pendant les repas.

phrases.text_local: À la gare du Nord, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: ah lah GAR doo NOR, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Replace 'la gare du Nord' with your destination. Having the address written down helps if pronunciation is tricky.

responses.phonetic: dah-KOR, mohn-TAY
responses.local: D'accord, montez.

responses.english: Do you have a specific address?
responses.phonetic: vooz ah-VAY oon ah-DRESS pray-SEEZ
responses.local: Vous avez une adresse précise ?

responses.phonetic: sah fuh-RAH ahn-vee-ROHN vahn mee-NOOT
responses.local: Ça fera environ vingt minutes.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how long the ride takes
phrases.text_local: C'est à combien de temps ?
phrases.pronunciation: seh tah kohm-BYEHN duh TAHN
phrases.cultural_note: Paris taxis use meters by law. Avoid unmarked cars offering rides at airports.

responses.english: About twenty minutes, depending on traffic.
responses.phonetic: ahn-vee-ROHN vahn mee-NOOT, suh-LOHN luh trah-FEEK
responses.local: Environ vingt minutes, selon le trafic.

responses.english: Tonight there's quite a bit of traffic.
responses.phonetic: suh SWAHR eel ee ah pah MAL duh seer-koo-lah-SYOHN
responses.local: Ce soir il y a pas mal de circulation.

phrases.text_local: Vous prenez la carte bancaire ?
phrases.pronunciation: voo pruh-NAY lah KART bahn-KAIR
phrases.cultural_note: Most taxis accept cards, but some smaller-town taxis may prefer cash. Always ask before the ride.

responses.phonetic: wee, sahn proh-BLEM
responses.local: Oui, sans problème.

responses.phonetic: day-zoh-LAY, es-PES suhl-MAHN
responses.local: Désolé, espèces seulement.

phrases.text_local: Arrêtez-vous ici, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: ah-reh-TAY voo ee-SEE, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: You can also point and say 'Ici, c'est parfait' (here is perfect). Taxis must drop you at a safe spot.

responses.phonetic: dah-KOR, zhuh mah-RET ee-SEE
responses.local: D'accord, je m'arrête ici.

responses.phonetic: sah feh vahn-DUH ZUH-roh
responses.local: Ça fait vingt-deux euros.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the driver to turn on the meter
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous allumer le compteur, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY voo ah-loo-MAY luh kohm-TUR, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: All licensed French taxis must run the meter. If a driver doesn't start it at the beginning of the journey, politely ask them to. Fixed fares (e.g., airport to Paris centre) are officially posted and don't require a meter.

responses.phonetic: WEE, byahn SOOR
responses.local: Oui, bien sûr.

responses.english: It's a fixed fare to the airport.
responses.phonetic: seh uhn tah-REEF feeks poor lah-ay-roh-POR
responses.local: C'est un tarif fixe pour l'aéroport.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to take the fastest route
phrases.text_local: Prenez le chemin le plus rapide, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: pruh-NAY luh shuh-MAN luh ploo rah-PEED, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Useful if you're in a hurry. Most Parisian taxi drivers use GPS navigation, but specifying 'le plus rapide' makes your preference clear. If you'd prefer to avoid tolls (péages), you can add 'sans péage'.

responses.phonetic: dah-KOR, zhuh PRAHN luh zhay-pay-ES
responses.local: D'accord, je prends le GPS.

responses.english: There's a lot of traffic right now.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah boh-KOO duh trah-FEEK ahn suh moh-MAHN
responses.local: Il y a beaucoup de trafic en ce moment.

phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous mettre l'air conditionné, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY voo MET-ruh lair kohn-dee-syoh-NAY, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Air conditioning is standard in French licensed taxis. In summer heat it's a completely normal request. You can also ask to open the window — 'pouvez-vous ouvrir la fenêtre' — if you prefer.

responses.phonetic: byahn SOOR, vwah-LAH
responses.local: Bien sûr, voilà.

responses.phonetic: ehl eh day-ZHAH ah-loo-MAY
responses.local: Elle est déjà allumée.

phrases.intent_en: Say you need to be somewhere by a specific time
phrases.text_local: J'ai besoin d'être là pour… heures, c'est possible ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhay buh-ZWAHN DET-ruh lah poor... UR, seh poh-SEE-bluh
phrases.cultural_note: Give the hour clearly and the driver will usually tell you honestly if it's achievable. Taxis in Paris use waze or Google Maps and can give a realistic estimate — asking early in the journey is best.

responses.english: Yes, we should make it in time.
responses.phonetic: WEE, ohn duh-VRAY ee ET-ruh ah TAHN
responses.local: Oui, on devrait y être à temps.

responses.phonetic: seh ZHOOS, meh ohn vah es-say-YAY
responses.local: C'est juste, mais on va essayer.

phrases.text_local: Je voudrais un reçu, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DREH uhn ruh-SOO, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Licensed taxis in France must provide a receipt on request. It will show the journey distance, fare, and the driver's licence number — useful for expense claims or if you've left something in the cab.

responses.phonetic: vwah-LAH VOH-truh ruh-SOO
responses.local: Voilà votre reçu.

responses.phonetic: zhuh voo lahm-PREEM
responses.local: Je vous l'imprime.

phrases.intent_en: Check in for a booked tour
phrases.text_local: Bonjour, j'ai une réservation pour la visite.
phrases.pronunciation: bon-ZHOOR, zhay ewn ray-zair-vah-SYON poor lah vee-ZEET
phrases.cultural_note: Most major champagne houses and wineries require advance booking — don't show up without one in peak season.

responses.english: Welcome! What name is it under?
responses.phonetic: byehn-vuh-NOO! seh tah kel NOHN
responses.local: Bienvenue ! C'est à quel nom ?

responses.english: The tour starts in ten minutes — make yourself comfortable.
responses.phonetic: lah vee-ZEET koh-MAHNSS dahn DEE mee-NOOT, ahn-stah-LAY voo
responses.local: La visite commence dans dix minutes, installez-vous.

phrases.text_local: La visite comprend une dégustation ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah vee-ZEET kom-PRON ewn day-gews-tah-SYON
phrases.cultural_note: Most house tours include 1–2 glasses at the end. Premium tastings cost extra. Always confirm what's included before paying.

responses.english: Yes, two glasses are included at the end.
responses.phonetic: wee, DUH VAIR sohn ahn-KLOO ah lah FAHN
responses.local: Oui, deux verres sont inclus à la fin.

responses.english: For a premium tasting it's twenty euros extra.
responses.phonetic: poor oon day-gews-tah-SYOHN pray-MYOM seh VAHN ZUH-roh duh PLOO
responses.local: Pour une dégustation premium c'est vingt euros de plus.

phrases.text_local: Il fait quelle température dans les caves ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel fay kel tem-pay-rah-TOOR don lay KAV
phrases.cultural_note: Champagne cellars stay at around 10–12°C year-round. Bring a light layer even in summer — guides will often warn you, but it's easy to forget.

responses.english: It's about twelve degrees — bring a jacket.
responses.phonetic: eel feh ahn-vee-ROHN DOOZ duh-GRAY, pruh-NAY oon VEST
responses.local: Il fait environ douze degrés, prenez une veste.

responses.english: We have gilets to lend if you'd like.
responses.phonetic: ohn ah day zhee-LEH ah pray-TAY see voo voo-LAY
responses.local: On a des gilets à prêter si vous voulez.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to taste a specific style
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que je peux goûter un blanc de blancs ?
phrases.pronunciation: es-kuh zhuh puh goo-TAY un blon duh BLON
phrases.cultural_note: Blanc de blancs = 100% Chardonnay (lighter, crisper). Blanc de noirs = made from red grapes Pinot Noir and/or Pinot Meunier (richer). Rosé = either blended or saignée method.

responses.english: Of course — it's our flagship cuvée.
responses.phonetic: byehn SOOR, seh noh-truh koo-VAY FAR
responses.local: Bien sûr, c'est notre cuvée phare.

responses.english: We can compare it with the blanc de noirs if you like.
responses.phonetic: ohn puh kohm-pah-RAY ah-VEK luh blon duh NWAHR see voo voo-LAY
responses.local: On peut comparer avec le blanc de noirs si vous voulez.

phrases.text_local: Est-ce que vous vendez à la propriété ?
phrases.pronunciation: es-kuh voo von-DAY ah lah proh-pree-ay-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: Buying direct from the producer (à la propriété) is often cheaper and gives access to limited or older vintages not available in shops.

responses.english: Yes, you can buy in the shop after the tour.
responses.phonetic: wee, voo poo-VAY ash-TAY ahn boo-TEEK ah-PREH lah vee-ZEET
responses.local: Oui, vous pouvez acheter en boutique après la visite.

responses.english: We also ship abroad if you'd like.
responses.phonetic: ohn ex-pay-DEE oh-SEE ah lay-trahn-ZHAY see voo voo-LAY
responses.local: On expédie aussi à l'étranger si vous voulez.

phrases.text_local: C'est un millésimé ou un assemblage ?
phrases.pronunciation: say tun mee-lay-zee-MAY oo un ah-som-BLAHZH
phrases.cultural_note: Millésimé = vintage (from a single exceptional year). Assemblage / non-vintage = a blend of multiple years for consistent style. Most champagne is non-vintage.

responses.english: It's a blend of three different years.
responses.phonetic: seh uhn ah-som-BLAHZH duh TRWAH ah-NAY dee-fay-RAHNT
responses.local: C'est un assemblage de trois années différentes.

responses.english: This one is a 2018 vintage — exceptional.
responses.phonetic: suh-LWEE-LAH seh uhn mee-lay-zee-MAY duh-meel-dee-ZWEET, ex-sep-syoh-NEL
responses.local: Celui-là c'est un millésimé 2018, exceptionnel.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to store a bottle
phrases.text_local: Comment conserver cette bouteille à la maison ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MON kon-sair-VAY set boo-TAY ah lah may-ZON
phrases.cultural_note: Keep champagne horizontal in a cool dark place (10–15°C). Non-vintage should be drunk within 3–5 years; vintage can age 10–20+ years.

responses.english: On its side, away from light, between ten and fifteen degrees.
responses.phonetic: ah PLAH, ah lah-BREE duh lah loo-MYAIR, ahn-truh DEE ay KANZ duh-GRAY
responses.local: À plat, à l'abri de la lumière, entre dix et quinze degrés.

responses.english: This non-vintage should be drunk within three years.
responses.phonetic: suh nohn-mee-lay-zee-MAY suh BWAH dahn lay TRWAH AHN
responses.local: Ce non-millésimé se boit dans les trois ans.

phrases.text_local: C'est vraiment excellent. Félicitations à toute l'équipe.
phrases.pronunciation: say vray-MON ek-sel-LON. fay-lee-see-tah-SYON ah toot lay-KEEP
phrases.cultural_note: French winemakers and cellar guides appreciate genuine enthusiasm. A heartfelt compliment goes a long way and often leads to a bonus pour.

responses.english: Thank you so much — that means a lot to us!
responses.phonetic: mair-SEE boh-KOO, sah noo feh treh play-ZEER
responses.local: Merci beaucoup, ça nous fait très plaisir !

responses.english: I'll pass that on to the whole team.
responses.phonetic: zhuh veh trahns-MET-ruh ah toot lay-KEEP
responses.local: Je vais transmettre à toute l'équipe.

phrases.intent_en: Buy a lift ticket / day pass
phrases.text_local: Un forfait de la journée, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: uhn for-FEH duh lah joor-NAY, seel voo plEH
phrases.cultural_note: French ski resorts sell 'forfait journalier' (day pass). Book online for discounts.

responses.phonetic: ah-DOOLT oo ahn-FAHN
responses.local: Adulte ou enfant?

responses.phonetic: ah-VEK ah-sy-RANS
responses.local: Avec assurance?

phrases.text_local: Où est la location de skis?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh lah loh-kah-SYON duh skee
phrases.cultural_note: Rental shops ('location') are everywhere at resorts. Book ahead in peak season.

responses.phonetic: skee oo snoh-BOHRD
responses.local: Skis ou snowboard?

responses.phonetic: kel TAY
responses.local: Quelle taille?

phrases.text_local: Où sont les cours de ski?
phrases.pronunciation: oo son lay KOOR duh skee
phrases.cultural_note: ESF (École du Ski Français) runs lessons at most resorts. Group or private options.

responses.phonetic: groop oo pree-VAY
responses.local: Groupe ou privé?

responses.phonetic: kel nee-VOH
responses.local: Quel niveau?

phrases.text_local: Quelle est la difficulté de cette piste?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh lah deee-fee-KOOL-teh duh set peest
phrases.cultural_note: French slopes are color-coded: green (easy), blue (intermediate), red (advanced), black (expert).

responses.phonetic: BLUH, roozh oo nwar
responses.local: Bleue, rouge ou noire?

responses.phonetic: day-bu-TAHN, an-ter-meh-dee-YAIR oo eks-PEHR
responses.local: Débutant, intermédiaire ou expert?

phrases.text_local: Quel est le bulletin d'avalanche?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh luh buh-leh-TAN dah-vah-LANSH
phrases.cultural_note: Always check the avalanche bulletin ('bulletin d'avalanche') before heading off-piste.

responses.phonetic: risk treh soor sink oh-zhoor-DWE
responses.local: Risque 3 sur 5 aujourd'hui.

responses.phonetic: ah-tahn-SYON, or-peest dan-zhuh-RUH
responses.local: Attention, hors-piste dangereux.

phrases.intent_en: Order hot chocolate at mountain hut
phrases.text_local: Deux chocolats chauds, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: duh shoh-koh-LAH sho, seel voo plEH
phrases.cultural_note: Mountain huts ('refuges') serve excellent hot chocolate. Expect a break from skiing.

responses.phonetic: ah-VEK oo sah krehm
responses.local: Avec ou sans crème?

responses.phonetic: ah-VEE ghee-MOHV
responses.local: Avec guimauves?

phrases.text_local: Où est le meilleur après-ski?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh luh meh-YUR ah-PREH-skee
phrases.cultural_note: French après-ski is legendary. Ask locals for the best spots — it varies by resort.

responses.english: The Snow Bar is the best!
responses.phonetic: luh bar day NEZH eh top
responses.local: Le Bar des Neiges est top!

responses.phonetic: lah foh-LEE doos, byehn soor
responses.local: La Folie Douce, bien sûr!

phrases.intent_en: Report an accident / ask for first aid
phrases.text_local: Il y a eu un accident! Appelez les secours!
phrases.pronunciation: eel y ah oo un ahk-see-DAN! ah-peh-LAY luh seh-KOOR
phrases.cultural_note: Emergency number for mountains: 112 (all of Europe) or PGHM (helicopter rescue).

responses.phonetic: oo teh-VOO
responses.local: Où êtes-vous?

responses.phonetic: zhahn-VWAH luh seh-KOOR
responses.local: J'envoie les secours.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about ski bus/shuttle to resort
phrases.text_local: Où passe la navette pour la station?
phrases.pronunciation: oo pas lah nah-VET poor lah stah-SYON
phrases.cultural_note: Many French resorts have free ski buses ('navettes') connecting villages to lifts.

responses.phonetic: toot leh KINZ mee-NOOT
responses.local: Toutes les 15 minutes.

responses.phonetic: grah-TOO-ee poor leh skee-YUR
responses.local: Gratuit pour les skieurs.

phrases.text_local: Où est le consigne à skis?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh luh kon-SEE-ny ah skee
phrases.cultural_note: Most resorts have ski storage ('consigne') near lifts. Lockers cost 5-15 euros/day.

responses.phonetic: ah gohsh, preh duh-kuh-YUH
responses.local: À gauche, près de l'accueil.

responses.phonetic: eht ooh-roh par zhoor
responses.local: 8 euros par jour.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about snow conditions / open slopes
phrases.text_local: Quelles pistes sont ouvertes aujourd'hui?
phrases.pronunciation: kel peest son ower-teh oh-zhoor-DWE
phrases.cultural_note: Check the resort website or app for real-time slope status. Some high-altitude runs stay open year-round.

responses.phonetic: toot leh peest son tower
responses.local: Toutes les pistes sont ouvertes.

responses.phonetic: suhl-mahn leh BLUH
responses.local: Seulement les bleues.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about ski pass for children / family discount
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous un forfait famille?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY voo un for-FEH fah-MEE
phrases.cultural_note: Many French resorts offer family discounts. Children under 5 often ski free.

responses.phonetic: kom-bee-yen dahn-FAHN
responses.local: Combien d'enfants?

responses.phonetic: vahnt soor sahnt duh ray-dook-SYON
responses.local: 20% de réduction.
